untranslatable_reason: This signal requires options pricing calculations (calendar spread liquidating value, break-even analysis) which cannot be expressed using only OHLCV price action indicators. The rule demands: (1) estimation of option liquidating values at successive price points, (2) comparison to initial spread value plus commissions, and (3) identification of break-even points through options mathematics. These require derivatives pricing models (Black-Scholes or binomial), volatility term structure analysis, and time decay calculations—none of which are available in the allowed indicator set. The signal is a manual options analysis framework, not a tradeable technical condition.
translation_notes: McMillan's calendar spread break-even methodology is an analytical tool for options traders to manually compute profitability thresholds at expiration, not a mechanical entry/exit signal. It requires domain-specific options pricing knowledge and cannot be automated with price-only technical indicators.
